lesson: Unit 01 Studio — MUS Unit Test I (Lessons 1–10)
banner: Week 12 · Final Review + MUS Unit Test I
essential_question: Can you show ten lessons' worth of skill with nobody coaching you through it?
objective: Complete a final mixed review of Lessons 1–10, take Math-U-See Unit Test I independently, and reflect honestly on which lessons are mastered and which need reteaching.

content.prompt: Without notes, write one sentence each defining: point, angle, parallel lines, transversal, and triangle inequality. Then check against your concept notes.

agenda.title: Review — Last Look Before the Test

content.heading: Fix what you missed
content.note: Go back over any missed problems from the capstone build and this week's practice, focusing on identifying angle pairs and picking the right perimeter/area formula. Fix each one and note WHY you missed it.

content.heading: Show what you own — all ten lessons
On your own now
content.note: Take Math-U-See Unit Test I (Lessons 1–10) independently — no notes, no help. Put it in the folder for grading when you're done.

content.preview: Next week: Lesson 11, Polygons — angles, diagonals & regular polygons, starting Unit 02.

success_criteria.text: I can correctly answer mixed questions across Lessons 1–10 without help
I can complete MUS Unit Test I independently within one session
I can honestly rate my mastery of each of the ten lessons and name what needs reteaching

questions.prompt: Which best describes two lines that never meet and stay the same distance apart?
questions.explain: Parallel lines never intersect and keep a constant distance apart.

questions.prompt: An angle measuring exactly 90° is called a(n)…
Two angles are complementary. One is 37°. What is the other?
A quadrilateral with two pairs of parallel sides is best called a…
questions.explain: A parallelogram has two pairs of parallel sides; a trapezoid has only one.

questions.prompt: Classify a triangle with angles 40°, 40°, 100° by both its sides and its angles (you may assume it's isosceles by construction).
questions.answer: Isosceles (two equal angles mean two equal sides) and obtuse (one angle is over 90°).
questions.explain: Two congruent angles (40°, 40°) mean two congruent sides (isosceles); a 100° angle makes it obtuse.

questions.prompt: Close: after MUS Unit Test I, rate your mastery of Unit 01 (Emerging / Proficient / Mastered) and name the ONE lesson from 1–10 you'd want to revisit before it comes up again.
